app.title: Cisco Configurator
app.subtitle: Create and validate IOS / IOS-XE configurations in a structured workflow
text.setzt_die_akzentfarbe_auf_orange_zu: Resets the accent color to orange.
text.der_gegenstellenentwurf_wurde_als_p: The peer draft was added as a project device. Placeholders <...> must be reviewed before transfer.
text.fur_dieses_projektgerat_ist_noch_ke: No configuration has been stored for this project device yet.
text.aktuelles_projekt_verwerfen_und_ein: Discard the current project and start a new project?
text.korrekturen_wurden_angewendet_platz: Fixes were applied. Placeholders and security-relevant values must be reviewed.
text.quelle_und_ziel_mussen_ausgewahlt_w: Source and target must be selected.
text.quelle_und_ziel_durfen_nicht_identi: Source and target must not be identical.
text.diese_verbindung_ist_bereits_im_pro: This connection already exists in the project.
text.vorschau_wurde_in_die_zwischenablag: Preview was copied to the clipboard.
text.konfiguration_wurde_uber_den_gewahl: Configuration was sent through the selected COM port.
text.es_wurde_ein_autosave_gefunden_wied: An autosave was found. Restore it?
text.sprache_startverhalten_und_grundleg: Language, startup behavior, and basic safety confirmations.
text.design_und_informationsdichte_der_b: Theme and information density of the user interface.
text.standardwerte_und_aufbau_der_erzeug: Defaults and structure of the generated Cisco configuration.
text.dateinamen_zielordner_und_zusatzlic: File names, destination folders, and additional export artifacts.
text.standardwerte_fur_geratezugriff_ube: Defaults for device access, transfer, and backups.
text.darstellung_der_topologie_und_vorga: Topology display and defaults for project reports.
text.zusatzliche_prufungen_und_technisch: Additional checks and technical diagnostic options.
settings.title: Manage settings
settings.subtitle: Manage language, appearance, configuration, export, backup, SSH, diagram, and report defaults in one place.
settings.language_note: The language changes without restarting. Cisco commands and internal configuration values are not translated.
text.entwicklermodus_zeigt_interne_modul: Developer mode shows internal module and field IDs in tooltips, logs missing translations, and enables translation and diagnostic checks. It does not change the generated Cisco configuration.
text.diagnosedetails_erganzen_fehlermeld: Diagnostic details add technical information such as stack traces to errors and logs. The option can be disabled for normal use.
text.die_strenge_importprufung_meldet_un: Strict import validation reports incomplete, conflicting, or ambiguous commands in more detail.
text.die_ubersetzungsprufung_ist_nur_im_: The translation audit is only available in developer mode.
text.keine_unubersetzten_sichtbaren_text: No untranslated visible text was found.
text.verwaltet_mehrere_router_und_switch: Manage multiple routers and switches with shared IPAM, link, backup, and report data.
text.beim_speichern_werden_gerate_ipam_v: Saving stores devices, IPAM, connections, and backups together in a .ciscoproject.json file.
text.zentrale_ipv4_ipv6_netzplanung_mit_: Central IPv4/IPv6 network planning with VLAN, gateway, DHCP range, device, and interface.
text.erzeugt_aus_der_aktuellen_oder_gesp: Creates a tabular port overview from the current or stored configuration.
text.pruft_modulbeziehungen_fehlende_pfl: Checks module dependencies, missing required values, VLAN/trunk consistency, and routing prerequisites.
text.analysiert_die_erzeugte_konfigurati: Analyzes the generated configuration for common management, password, SNMP, STP, and VLAN risks.
text.vergleicht_zwei_konfigurationen_kon: Compares two configurations by context and generates a rollback configuration.
text.findet_einstellungen_und_cisco_befe: Finds settings and Cisco commands and navigates directly to the relevant input field.
text.ordnet_eine_befehlszeile_dem_regist: Assigns a command line to a registry entry and explains command keywords and parameter positions.
text.ubertragt_konfigurationen_per_opens: Transfers configurations using an OpenSSH key or Plink password and manages running/startup backups.
text.passworter_werden_weder_im_projekt_: Passwords are not stored in the project or autosave.
text.gerate_konnen_mit_der_maus_verschob: Devices can be moved with the mouse. Connection type, interfaces, and an optional description are shown on the link and stored in the project.
text.verbindungstyp_der_typ_steuert_farb: Connection type. The type controls the color and line style in the diagram.
text.optionale_bezeichnung_z_b_ospf_tran: Optional label, for example OSPF transit, MPLS core, or internet uplink.
text.verwirft_manuelle_positionen_und_or: Discards manual positions and rearranges all devices.
text.gerate_per_drag_drop_verschieben_ve: Move devices by drag and drop  ·  Connection types:
text.erzeugt_gerateubersicht_ip_plan_ver: Generates a device overview, IP plan, connections, checks, and test plan as HTML, DOCX, or PDF.
text.keine_uberschneidungen_oder_ungulti: No overlaps or invalid addresses were detected.
text.aktualisierte_cisco_konfigurations_: Search updated Cisco configuration, diagnostic, validation, and maintenance commands.
text.zeigt_alle_befehle_oder_nur_die_bef: Shows all commands or only commands from a specific group.
text.durchsucht_modul_modus_befehl_und_b: Searches module, mode, command, and description.
text.unterstutzt_ipv4_und_ipv6_automatis: Automatically supports IPv4 and IPv6 based on the entered address. Enter a network, set the target prefix, and review the calculated subnets on the right.
text.pruft_ipv4_oder_ipv6_berechnet_das_: Validates IPv4 or IPv6, calculates the associated network, and displays the address range, prefix mask, and protocol-specific information.
text.aktiviere_links_ein_modul_die_zugeh: Enable a module on the left. Its inputs will then appear here.
text.durchsucht_die_module_dieses_bereic:
Searches the modules in this section.

The search covers module names, internal module IDs, field names, and help text.
text.stp_modul_aktivieren_um_die_live_vo: Enable the STP module to display the live preview.
text.vorschau_konnte_nicht_erzeugt_werde: The preview could not be generated
text.fur_die_aktuellen_angaben_wurden_ke: No STP commands were generated for the current values.
text.aktive_module_zusammenfuhren_prufen: Combine active modules, validate, copy, or transfer them directly through the serial console.
text.keine_detailbeschreibung_hinterlegt: No detailed description is available. Complete this field only if the command is required for the target configuration.
text.jede_zeile_ist_ein_eigener_eintrag_: Each line is a separate entry. Columns are separated with |.
text.weitere_befehle_im_tab_befehle: additional commands in the Commands tab.
text.optionaler_befehlsanteil_nur_setzen: optional command part; use it only when required or supported by IOS.
text.durchsucht_die_aktuell_sichtbare_ob: Scans the currently visible interface, including tooltips, for untranslated German text and writes the result to the diagnostics folder.
text.offnet_den_ordner_mit_entwicklerpro: Opens the folder containing the developer log, translation audit, and technical diagnostic information.
text.fur_die_ubersetzungsprufung_muss_di: Set the application language to English and apply the settings before running the translation audit.
text.logdatei_statt_echter_com_port: log file instead of a physical COM port
text.dieses_modul_passt_nicht_zum_aktuel: This module is not applicable to the current device type or VRF mode.
text.dieses_modul_ist_nicht_aktiv_und_wi: This module is not enabled and will not be exported.
text.modul_aktiv_keine_offensichtlichen_: Module enabled. No obvious required-field warnings detected.
text.keine_module_aktiv_es_wird_praktisc: No modules are enabled. Almost no configuration will be generated.
text.offne_den_bereich_ausgabe_und_prufe: Open the Output section and review the duplicate notice shown there.
text.durchsucht_modul_modus_befehl_und_b:
Searches module, mode, command, and description.

Examples:
OSPF
VRF
interface
ssh
<ACL>
route-map
text.der_entwicklermodus_ist_ausschlie_l: Developer mode is intended exclusively for troubleshooting and translation audits. It displays internal IDs in tooltips, writes developer.log and translation_audit.txt, and does not modify Cisco commands or the generated configuration.
text.gerategrunddaten_zeiteinstellungen_: Configure device identity, time settings, and banners in one place.
text.administrationszugange_benutzer_aaa: Configure administrative access, users, AAA, and monitoring.
text.mpls_label_oder_label_protokoll_abh: MPLS label or label protocol, depending on the command.
text.lokale_ospf_prozess_id_muss_nicht_z: Local OSPF process ID; it does not need to match between devices.
text.mtu_ist_plattformabhangig_fur_qinq_: ! MTU is platform-dependent. QinQ does not automatically require an MTU of 9000.
text.erzeugt_die_cisco_konfiguration_anh:
Generates the Cisco configuration from the currently configured values and modules.

The preview is not displayed permanently below the input form.
text.zweck_allgemeine_globale_routingfun:
Purpose:
Configure general global routing functions.

Generated commands:
ip routing or no ip routing.

Note:
This command is particularly relevant on Layer 3 switches and is no longer treated as a custom command during import.
text.zweck_ospfv2_ohne_vrf_fur_ipv4_konf:
Purpose:
Configure OSPFv2 for IPv4 without VRF.

Generated commands:
router ospf, router-id, log-adjacency-changes, network ... area ... or interface-based ip ospf <process> area <area>.

Note:
The configuration method can be process-based, interface-based, or mixed.
text.zweck_grundidentitat_basisschutz_un:
Purpose:
Configure the device identity, baseline security, and time zone.

Generated commands:
hostname, ip domain-name, enable secret, no ip domain-lookup, service password-encryption, clock timezone, and clock summer-time.

Note:
The domain name is particularly important for SSH/RSA key generation. Clock settings are intentionally located in the base section.
text.zweck_dynamische_spanning_tree_konf:
Purpose:
Provide dynamic Spanning Tree configuration for PVST+, Rapid-PVST+, and MST. After selecting a mode, only valid input sections are displayed and exported.

Generated commands:
Global STP options, VLAN root/priority/timers, interface profiles, PortFast, BPDU Guard/Filter, Root/Loop Guard, link type, cost, port priority, MST region/instances, errdisable recovery, and reset commands.

Checks:
Priority increments, VLAN/MST assignments, guard conflicts, port-channel consistency, MST region comparison, and platform notices.

Live preview:
The generated STP section is displayed directly in the module.
text.packet_tracer_unterstutzt_nicht_all: ! Packet Tracer does not support every IOS/IOS-XE command. Verify SNMPv3, IPsec, QoS, Zone Firewall, AAA/TACACS, and selected switch-security commands.
text.qinq_l3_subinterfaces_werden_beim_l: QinQ: Layer 3 subinterfaces are intentionally not generated for a Layer 2 switch.
text.vlan_1_wird_als_native_vlan_verwend: VLAN 1 is used as the native VLAN.
text.ipv6_benotigt_einen_prafix_zwischen: IPv6 requires a prefix between /0 and /128, for example /64.
text.der_hostname_ist_der_eindeutige_ger: The hostname is the unique device name shown in the CLI prompt. It improves documentation, troubleshooting, and syslog analysis and prevents confusion between multiple routers and switches.
text.der_domain_name_wird_unter_cisco_io: Cisco IOS requires a domain name before generating RSA keys for SSH. Example: example.local or company.local.
text.das_enable_secret_schutzt_den_privi: The enable secret protects privileged EXEC mode. It should always be used instead of enable password because it is stored more securely.
text.legt_eine_mindestlange_fur_neue_pas: Sets a minimum length for new passwords. Useful for hardening. Typical values are 10, 12, or higher.
text.verhindert_dns_lookups_bei_vertippt: Prevents DNS lookups for mistyped commands. Without this command, the router attempts to resolve typing errors as host names, which causes delays.
text.verschlusselt_einfache_passworter_i: Encrypts simple passwords in the configuration using type 7. This is not strong protection, but it prevents plain-text display of basic passwords.
text.kurzname_der_standardzeitzone_fur_d: Short name of the standard time zone for the `clock timezone` command, for example CET. The label and UTC offset are configured separately.
text.stundenabweichung_der_standardzeit_: UTC offset in hours for standard time. The default for Germany is 1. Negative values such as -5 are supported.
text.zusatzliche_minutenabweichung_gegen: Additional UTC offset in minutes. Usually 0; use values such as 30 for half-hour time zones.
text.kurzname_der_sommerzeit_fur_clock_s: Short name of the daylight-saving time zone for `clock summer-time`, for example CEST. Leave empty when daylight-saving time is not required.
text.vollstandiger_regelteil_nach_dem_so: Complete rule section after the daylight-saving-time name. Example: recurring last Sun Mar 2:00 last Sun Oct 3:00. A date-based IOS rule can be entered instead.
text.variable_company_wird_nur_im_banner: The {COMPANY} variable is replaced only when it is used in the banner text.
text.variable_location_sinnvoll_bei_mehr: Variable {LOCATION}. Useful when multiple sites or labs are involved.
text.variable_admin_hier_kann_eine_maila: Variable {ADMIN}. Enter an email address, telephone number, or operations contact.
text.motd_erscheint_vor_oder_beim_login_: The MOTD banner appears before or during login. Use it for legal notices, warnings, and contact information. Variables: {HOSTNAME}, {DATE}, {TIME}, {DEVICE_TYPE}, {LOCATION}, {ADMIN}, {COMPANY}.
text.login_banner_wird_beim_zugriff_auf_: The login banner is displayed when accessing the console or VTY lines. Useful for clear warnings and ownership information.
text.rsa_schlussellange_fur_ssh_2048_ist: RSA key length for SSH. 2048 bits is the standard value. Packet Tracer commonly accepts 1024 or 2048. Use 2048 bits or higher on real devices.
text.zeit_in_sekunden_wie_lange_eine_ssh: Maximum time in seconds that an SSH authentication attempt may remain open.
text.maximale_anzahl_authentifizierungsv: Maximum number of authentication attempts per SSH connection.
text.optionales_quellinterface_fur_ssh_z: Optional source interface for SSH, for example Vlan99 or Loopback0.
text.aktiviert_ssh_event_logging_falls_v: Enables SSH event logging when supported by the IOS/IOS-XE platform.
text.optional_nur_setzen_wenn_dein_ios_d: Optional. Configure this only if the target IOS version supports the command.
text.endnummer_der_vty_line_klassisch_0_: Last VTY line number. Traditional ranges use 0 4; many devices support 0 15.
text.login_local_nutzt_lokale_username_a: `login local` uses local username accounts. `login authentication default` uses AAA. `login` with a password uses the line password.
text.nur_bei_password_login_relevant_son: Relevant only when password login is used. Leave empty otherwise.
text.optional_access_class_fur_ssh_quell: Optional access class for permitted SSH source networks, for example 10 or MGMT-SSH.
text.automatische_abmeldung_in_minuten_s: Automatic logout in minutes and seconds, for example 10 0.
text.logging_synchronous_verhindert_dass: `logging synchronous` prevents console messages from interrupting command input.
text.optionales_privilege_level_auf_der_: Optional privilege level on the VTY line. Usually leave this empty and manage privileges through local users or AAA.
text.aktiviert_aaa_new_model_mit_lokaler: Enables aaa new-model with local authentication when no RADIUS or TACACS+ module is active. When RADIUS or TACACS+ is configured, the default method is generated centrally there to avoid conflicting aaa authentication login default commands.
text.automatische_abmeldung_auf_der_cons: Automatic console logout in minutes and seconds. Example: 10 0 disconnects the session after 10 minutes of inactivity.
text.optional_login_local_auf_console_se: Optional. login local on the console requires at least one local user. VTY and SSH settings are configured exclusively in the SSH module.
text.mehrere_radius_server_moglich_auth_: Multiple RADIUS servers are supported. Authentication normally uses port 1812 and accounting port 1813. Timeout and retransmit values are optional. Example: RAD1|192.168.99.10|1812|1813|Secret|5|3
text.mehrere_tacacs_server_moglich_tacac: Multiple TACACS+ servers are supported. TACACS+ separates authentication, authorization, and accounting and is particularly suitable for administrative access.
text.erzeugt_aaa_group_server_radius_und: Generates aaa group server radius and assigns the RADIUS servers to this group.
text.wenn_ja_wird_aaa_login_authorizatio: When set to Yes, AAA login, authorization, and accounting are generated for RADIUS with local fallback.
text.optional_quellinterface_fur_radius_: Optional source interface for RADIUS packets, for example Loopback0 or Vlan99. This provides a stable source address for firewalls and server communication.
text.ntp_synchronisiert_die_uhrzeit_wich: NTP synchronizes device time. Accurate time is important for syslog, certificates, troubleshooting, and event correlation. Format: Server IP|optional prefer.
text.syslog_sendet_logs_an_einen_zentral: Syslog sends log messages to a central server. Levels: emergencies, alerts, critical, errors, warnings, notifications, informational, and debugging.
text.snmpv3_ist_fur_monitoring_deutlich_: SNMPv3 is significantly more secure for monitoring than SNMPv2 because it supports authentication and encryption. Example: MON|snmpuser|sha|authpass|aes|privpass.
text.aktiviert_detaillierte_zeitstempel_: Enables detailed timestamps for log and debug messages.
text.gro_e_des_lokalen_logpuffers: Size of the local logging buffer.
text.loggt_erfolgreiche_und_fehlgeschlag: Logs successful and failed login attempts.
text.optionaler_speicherort_fur_konfigur: Optional destination for configuration archives, for example flash:/archive-$h or scp://user:pass@server/path. This uses the same archive configuration mode as configuration logging.
text.archiviert_die_konfiguration_bei_wr: Archives the configuration when `write memory` is executed, if supported by IOS/IOS-XE.
text.optionales_archivintervall_in_minut: Optional archive interval in minutes. Leave empty when configurations should be archived only manually.
text.zentrale_lokale_benutzerverwaltung_: Central local-user management. This list replaces the former separate user module so username commands are not generated twice. Privilege level 15 grants full access; lower levels are restricted.
text.optionale_parser_views_fur_eingesch: Optional parser views for restricted CLI permissions. Availability depends on IOS and is useful when users must not receive full administrative privileges.
text.routed_layer_3_port_mit_ip_adresse_: routed = Layer 3 port with an IP address. access = endpoint port in one VLAN. trunk = connection between switches or to a router or firewall carrying multiple VLANs.
text.konfiguriert_viele_ports_gleichzeit: Configures multiple ports at the same time. Use PortFast only on endpoint ports. BPDU Guard disables ports when an unexpected switch or BPDU is detected. Port security limits MAC addresses.
text.router_on_a_stick_ein_physisches_in: Router-on-a-Stick uses one physical interface to carry multiple VLANs through 802.1Q tags. The subinterface ID usually matches the VLAN ID, but this is not technically required.
text.physisches_router_interface_das_als: Physical router interface used as the trunk toward the switch. The connected switch port must also be configured as a trunk.
text.optionales_native_vlan_nur_eintrage: Optional native VLAN. Configure it only when untagged traffic is intentionally required. Usually leave it empty or use a dedicated unused VLAN.
text.setzt_no_shutdown_auf_dem_physische: Applies `no shutdown` to the physical parent interface. IP addresses are configured on the subinterfaces.
text.eine_zeile_pro_vlan_subinterface_es: One line per VLAN or subinterface. The module generates router subinterfaces with `interface Parent.VLAN`, `encapsulation dot1Q`, `ip address`, and `no shutdown`. The VLANs must already exist on the switch.
text.aktiviert_dscp_trust_auto_qos_trust: Enables DSCP trust or AutoQoS trust on interfaces. Platform dependent.
text.erzeugt_einfache_class_map_policy_m: Generates a simple class-map and policy-map structure.
text.profile_access_voice_trunk_uplink_s: Profiles: access, voice, trunk, uplink, server, AP, unused, and routed. Value1/Value2 represent VLANs, native VLANs, or IP address/mask depending on the profile.
text.erzeugt_saubere_trunk_uplink_konfig: Generates a clean trunk or uplink configuration with `nonegotiate` and optional Root Guard or Loop Guard.
text.namensprafix_fur_automatisch_erzeug:
Name prefix for automatically generated provider or service VLANs. Example: QINQ_SVLAN_200.

Command components:
vlan <Provider-VLAN> = creates the outer service VLAN.
name <Prefix><Provider-VLAN> = assigns a unique name to the S-VLAN.
text.optional_und_plattformabhangig_qinq:
Optional and platform-dependent. QinQ adds 4 bytes of overhead for every additional 802.1Q tag, so a value of 9000 is not universally required.

Practical guidance:
Leave empty = do not generate an MTU command.
1522/1526 = often sufficient for normal Ethernet plus VLAN/QinQ overhead, depending on the platform.
9216 = jumbo/provider-core scenario, only when supported end to end.
9000 is a host jumbo-frame value and is no longer used as the default.

Command when explicitly configured:
system mtu jumbo <Bytes>

Note: Many Catalyst platforms require a reload afterwards.
text.optionaler_hinweis_schalter_klassis:
Optional guidance switch. Classic Catalyst configurations normally use `switchport mode dot1q-tunnel` per port. Global dot1q and EtherType options depend heavily on the IOS/IOS-XE release and platform, so no arbitrary command is generated.

Validate the relevant platform syntax, for example `dot1q tunneling ethertype <0x8100|0x88A8>`.
text.portbasiertes_qinq_fur_customer_fac:
Port-based QinQ for customer-facing switchports. The port is assigned to a provider/service VLAN and operates in dot1q-tunnel mode.

Command components:
interface <Interface> = customer-facing port at the provider or switch edge.
description <Description> = port documentation.
switchport mode dot1q-tunnel = enables QinQ tunnel mode.
switchport access vlan <Provider-VLAN> = outer S-VLAN/provider VLAN.
l2protocol-tunnel <cdp|stp|vtp> = tunnels selected Layer 2 protocols when supported by the platform and IOS.

Interface MTU is no longer generated by default. MTU is managed globally through the optional system MTU because mtu <Bytes> is frequently platform-dependent or invalid on Layer 2 switchports.

Format: Interface|Provider-VLAN|Description|L2ProtocolTunnel.
L2ProtocolTunnel may contain cdp, stp, vtp, or all.
text.nur_l3_switch_parent_subid_provider: only L3-Switch: Parent|SubID|provider VLAN|Customer-VLAN|IP optional|mask optional|description
text.double_tagged_layer_3_subinterfaces:
Double-tagged Layer 3 subinterfaces are generated only for Layer 3 switches. They are intentionally skipped for Layer 2 switches, and the QinQ module is not offered for routers.

Command components:
`interface <Parent>.<SubID>` = subinterface on a routed parent interface.
`encapsulation dot1Q <Provider VLAN> second-dot1q <Customer VLAN>` = outer S-VLAN and inner C-VLAN.
`ip address <IP> <Mask>` = optional Layer 3 termination on the subinterface.

Format: Parent|Sub-ID|Provider VLAN|Customer VLAN|Optional IP|Optional mask|Description.
text.optional_port_channel_load_balance_: Optional port-channel load-balancing method, depending on the platform.
text.lacp_active_passive_pagp_desirable_: Supports LACP active/passive, PAgP desirable/auto, or static on mode. Generates the member-interface range and the Port Channel interface.
text.zweck_zentrales_rollenmodell_fur_po:
Purpose: Central role model for interfaces.

Command behavior:
access generates switchport mode access, the access VLAN, spanning-tree portfast, and BPDU Guard.
voice additionally generates the voice VLAN.
trunk generates trunk mode, allowed VLANs, the native VLAN, and nonegotiate.
uplink additionally enables Root Guard.
ap generates PortFast trunk behavior.
pc-member generates channel-group <ID> mode active.
routed generates no switchport, optional ip vrf forwarding, and an IP address.
unused applies shutdown.

Format: Interface|Role|Description|Access VLAN|Voice VLAN|Allowed VLANs|Native VLAN|Port Channel|IP|Mask|VRF.
text.vlans_trennen_layer_2_broadcast_dom: VLANs separate Layer 2 broadcast domains. VLAN 1 should not be used as a production or management VLAN.
text.svi_switch_virtual_interface_auf_l2: An SVI is a Switch Virtual Interface. On a Layer 2 switch it provides the management IP. On a Layer 3 switch it commonly acts as the default gateway for clients in the VLAN.
text.nur_fur_layer_2_switches_relevant_d: Relevant only to Layer 2 switches. It allows the switch to reach management networks outside its local subnet, for example for SSH access from other networks.
text.daten_vlan_fur_clients_hinter_einem: Data VLAN for clients connected behind an IP phone. It is created as a normal VLAN and applied to phone ports with `switchport access vlan <VLAN-ID>`.
text.name_des_daten_vlans_dient_der_lesb: Name of the data VLAN. It improves readability in `show vlan brief` and in the documentation.
text.voice_vlan_fur_ip_telefone_wird_als:
The voice VLAN for IP phones is created as a VLAN and assigned to phone ports with `switchport voice vlan`.

Command:
switchport voice vlan <VLAN-ID>
text.name_des_voice_vlans_empfehlung_kur: Name of the voice VLAN. Use a short, unambiguous name such as VOICE, VOIP, or TELEPHONY.
text.cdp_hilft_cisco_ip_telefonen_das_vo:
CDP helps Cisco IP phones discover the voice VLAN automatically.

Command:
`cdp run`
text.lldp_ist_herstellerubergreifend_vie:
LLDP is vendor-neutral. Many phones use LLDP-MED to discover the voice VLAN.

Commands:
lldp run
lldp med-tlv-select network-policy
text.qos_behandlung_fur_telefonports_aut:
QoS handling for phone ports.

`auto qos voip cisco-phone` = Cisco AutoQoS for phone ports.
`mls qos trust dscp` = trusts DSCP markings.
`mls qos trust cos` = trusts CoS markings on 802.1Q frames.
text.name_des_dhcp_pools_fur_telefone_de: Name of the DHCP pool for phones. The pool is generated only when network, mask, and gateway are provided.
text.netzadresse_des_voice_vlans_fur_den:
Network address of the voice VLAN for the DHCP pool.

Command:
network <Network> <Mask>
text.subnetzmaske_des_voice_netzes_fur_d: Subnet mask of the voice network for the DHCP pool.
text.default_gateway_fur_telefone_in_der:
Default gateway for phones, normally the SVI or router address of the voice VLAN.

Command:
default-router <Gateway-IP>
text.optionaler_dns_server_fur_telefone_: Optional DNS server for phones. Leave empty when DNS should not be distributed through DHCP.
text.optionaler_tftp_callmanager_server_:
Optional TFTP or CallManager server for phones. Cisco phones commonly use DHCP option 150.

Command:
option 150 ip <TFTP-IP>
text.optionaler_dhcp_ausschlussbereich_f: Optional DHCP exclusion. Enter a start and end address or a single address. Leave empty when no addresses should be excluded.
text.eine_zeile_pro_telefonport_befehlst:
One line per phone port.

Command components:
interface <Interface> = target port.
description <Description> = port documentation.
switchport mode access = access port for phone and PC.
switchport access vlan <Access-VLAN> = data VLAN.
switchport voice vlan <Voice-VLAN> = voice VLAN.
spanning-tree portfast = accelerates port activation for endpoint devices.
spanning-tree bpduguard enable = protects against switches connected to the endpoint port.
auto qos voip cisco-phone or mls qos trust ... = QoS trust or AutoQoS.
PortSecurity Yes = sticky MAC plus restrict.

Format: Interface|Description|Access-VLAN|Voice-VLAN|PortFast|BPDU Guard|QoS|PortSecurity.

Device filter: This module is available only for Layer 2 and Layer 3 switches because switchport voice vlan is not a router-port command.
text.dhcp_snooping_schutzt_vor_fremden_d: DHCP snooping protects against unauthorized DHCP servers. Enter the VLANs in which clients use DHCP, for example 10,20,99.
text.dynamic_arp_inspection_schutzt_gege: Dynamic ARP Inspection protects against ARP spoofing. It requires a reliable DHCP-snooping binding table or static bindings.
text.erzeugt_typische_access_port_konfig: Generates a typical access-port configuration with PortFast, BPDU Guard, port security, storm control, and shutdown for unused ports.
text.zeit_bis_zur_automatischen_wiederak: Delay in seconds before automatic recovery attempts to re-enable an err-disabled interface.
text.typische_ursachen_bpduguard_psecure: Common causes include bpduguard, psecure-violation, udld, link-flap, and storm-control.
text.wahlt_das_aktive_spanning_tree_prot: Selects the active Spanning Tree protocol. The interface automatically displays only the options that apply to the selected mode.
text.steuert_kompatibilitatswarnungen_da: Controls compatibility warnings. The tool generates Cisco IOS/IOS-XE syntax; Packet Tracer may support advanced commands only partially.
text.optionales_modell_fur_dokumentation: Optional model name for documentation and compatibility checks, for example C9200L-24P-4G.
text.optionale_versionsangabe_z_b_17_12_: Optional version information, for example 17.12.4. It is used for guidance but does not independently change the target platform.
text.bei_ja_werden_protokollfremde_optio: When enabled, options that do not match the selected protocol are not exported and are reported as warnings.
text.wahlt_die_kurze_oder_lange_stp_pfad: Selects the short or long STP path-cost scale.
text.aktiviert_portfast_global_fur_geeig: Enables PortFast globally for suitable access ports.
text.aktiviert_bpdu_guard_global_fur_por: Enables BPDU Guard globally for PortFast-ports.
text.aktiviert_bpdu_filter_global_nur_ge: Enables BPDU Filter globally. Use it only deliberately because it can suppress BPDUs.
text.legacy_pvst_konvergenzoptimierung_n: Legacy PVST convergence optimization. It is not exported for Rapid PVST or MST.
text.legacy_pvst_optimierung_fur_indirek: Legacy PVST optimization for indirect link failures. Do not export it for Rapid-PVST or MST.
text.ein_vlan_oder_bereich_z_b_10_oder_1: A VLAN or VLAN range, for example 10 or 10,20,30-40.
text.schnellassistent_fur_root_primary_r: Quick assistant for Root Primary, Root Secondary, or a manual bridge priority.
text.nur_gultige_cisco_werte_in_schritte: Only valid Cisco values in increments of 4096 can be selected.
text.beispiel_10_primary_pro_zeile_wird_: Example: 10|primary. One root role is generated per line.
text.beispiel_20_8192_erlaubt_sind_0_bis: Example: 20|8192. Valid values range from 0 to 61440 in increments of 4096.
text.beispiel_gi1_0_1_10_64_10000_port_p: Example: Gi1/0/1|10|64|10000. Port priority ranges from 0 to 240 in increments of 16.
text.profile_client_server_access_point_: Profiles: Client, Server, Access Point, Switch Uplink, Port Channel, and Provider Uplink. VLAN columns are used for documentation and plausibility checks.
text.portfast_ja_trunk_oder_nein_guard_r: PortFast: Yes, trunk, or No. Guard: root, loop, or empty. Link type is exported only for Rapid-PVST or MST.
text.ein_interface_pro_zeile_root_guard_: One interface per line. Root Guard prevents a superior root bridge from being elected through this port.
text.ein_interface_pro_zeile_loop_guard_: One interface per line. Loop Guard protects against unidirectional Spanning Tree failures.
text.muss_auf_allen_switches_derselben_m: The name and revision must be identical on all switches in the same MST region.
text.revisionsnummer_0_bis_65535_sie_mus: Revision number from 0 to 65535. It must be identical throughout an MST region.
text.erzeugt_spanning_tree_mst_max_hops_: Generates spanning-tree mst max-hops. The usual valid range is 1 through 40.
text.instanz_1_bis_4094_instanz_0_ist_di: Instances range from 1 to 4094. Instance 0 is the IST/CIST and is not used for a separate VLAN mapping.
text.schnellassistent_fur_root_rolle_ode: Quick assistant for a root role or manual instance priority.
text.nur_gultige_werte_in_schritten_von_: Only valid values in increments of 4096.
text.vergleicht_andere_switches_mit_der_: Compares other switches with the local region. Example: SW2|COMPANY|1|1:10,20;2:30-40|ABCD.
text.wird_zusammen_mit_bpdu_guard_recove: Generated together with BPDU Guard recovery. A typical value is 300 seconds.
text.scopes_global_vlan_mst_interface_be: Scopes: global, VLAN, MST, and interface. Examples: VLAN|10|priority or interface|Gi1/0/1|portfast.
text.zeigt_ausschlie_lich_den_aktuell_er: Displays only the currently generated STP configuration section. Hidden fields are not exported when they do not apply to the selected mode.
text.dhcp_snooping_dai_ip_source_guard: DHCP Snooping / DAI / IP Source Guard
text.uplinks_dhcp_server_ports_denen_dhc: Uplinks or DHCP-server ports on which DHCP and ARP traffic is trusted.
text.dhcp_snooping_rate_limit_pro_interf: Configures a DHCP Snooping rate limit per interface.
text.aktiviert_ip_verify_source_pro_inte: Enables `ip verify source` on each listed interface.
text.erweiterte_port_security_je_interfa: Advanced Port Security settings per interface.
text.vlan_name_ipv4_netz_maske_gateway_i: VLAN|Name|IPv4 network|Mask|Gateway|IPv6 gateway|VRF|DHCP Yes/No|DNS|Helper|HSRP VIP|OSPF area|BGP Yes/No
text.zweck_vlan_ip_adressplan_zentral_pf:
Purpose:
Maintain the VLAN and IP addressing plan centrally.

Generated commands:
vlan <ID>, name <Name>, interface Vlan<ID>, ip vrf forwarding <VRF>, ip address <Gateway> <Mask>, ipv6 address, ip helper-address, standby, and ip dhcp pool.

Command behavior:
VRF is applied before the IP address.
DHCP = Yes generates a DHCP pool.
Helper generates `ip helper-address`.
HSRP VIP generates `standby <VLAN> ip <VIP>`.
OSPF/BGP values are exported as comments for documentation.
text.aktiviert_mit_ip_routing_das_ipv4_r: Enables IPv4 routing on a Layer 3 switch with `ip routing`. Routing is normally already active on routers, but the command remains valid and can still be generated.
text.statische_routen_definieren_feste_w: Static routes define fixed paths. The administrative distance is optional; a higher value can create a floating static route that is used only when a dynamic or lower-distance route fails.
text.ospf_process_id_ist_nur_lokal_auf_d: The OSPF process ID is locally significant on this router and does not need to match neighboring routers. It identifies the local OSPF process.
text.prozessbasiert_erzeugt_network_area: Process-based mode generates `network ... area ...` in the OSPF process. Interface-based mode generates `ip ospf <Process> area <Area>` directly under the interfaces. Mixed mode permits both methods at the same time.
text.eindeutige_32_bit_id_im_ipv4_format: Unique 32-bit identifier in IPv4 format, for example 1.1.1.1. It identifies the OSPF router in LSAs, adjacencies, and the link-state database and must be unique within the OSPF domain.
text.referenzbandbreite_in_mbit_s_fur_di: Reference bandwidth in Mbit/s used for OSPF cost calculation. Example: 10000 for 10 Gbit/s. Configure the same value on all OSPF routers.
text.ja_erzeugt_log_adjacency_changes_de: Ja generated log-adjacency-changes. Detail generated log-adjacency-changes detail and protokolliert additional state changes the OSPF-adjacencies.
text.aktiviert_ospf_auf_interfaces_deren: Enables OSPF on interfaces whose IP address matches the configured range. Area 0 is the backbone. Interfaces on the same link must use the same area.
text.interfacebasierte_ospf_aktivierung_: Interface-based OSPF activation. One line per interface: Interface|Area. Example: GigabitEthernet1/0/48|0 generates `ip ospf <process-id> area 0` under the interface.
text.passive_interface_default_schutzt_v: passive-interface default prevents unintended OSPF adjacencies. Add no passive-interface only for the interfaces that should form neighbors.
text.ja_erzeugt_default_information_orig: Yes generates default-information originate. Always generates default-information originate always and advertises the default route even when no local 0.0.0.0/0 route exists.
text.interfaces_auf_denen_ospf_nachbarn_: Interfaces that are allowed to form OSPF neighbor adjacencies.
text.lokaler_prozessname_interfaces_werd: Local process name. Interfaces are assigned to this IS-IS process later. The tag does not need to be identical across the network.
text.network_entity_title_identifiziert_: Network Entity Title identifying the IS-IS router. It must be unique. Example: 49.0001.0000.0000.0001.00.
text.is_is_wird_interfacebezogen_aktivie: IS-IS is enabled per interface. Enter one interface name per line, for example GigabitEthernet0/0.
text.autonomous_system_nummer_des_lokale: Autonomous system number of the local router. The same AS on the peer indicates iBGP; a different AS indicates eBGP.
text.eindeutige_bgp_router_id_sie_sollte: Unique BGP router ID. It should remain stable; a loopback address is commonly used. It is important for BGP identification and troubleshooting.
text.bgp_baut_keine_nachbarn_automatisch: BGP does not discover neighbors automatically. Each neighbor must be configured explicitly with its IP address and remote AS number.
text.bgp_announced_nur_netze_die_passend: BGP advertises only networks that exist in the routing table with a matching prefix. Format: network|mask.
text.optionale_erweiterungen_je_neighbor: Optional settings per neighbor. Each entry must match a neighbor from the list above.
text.hsrp_stellt_eine_virtuelle_gateway_: HSRP provides a virtual gateway IP address. Clients use the VIP as their default gateway. The higher priority wins, and preempt allows the router to resume the active role after recovery.
text.ordnet_interfaces_einer_vrf_zu_acht: Assigns interfaces to a VRF. Caution: ip vrf forwarding removes an existing IP address on real devices.
text.route_map_mit_optionalem_match_pref: Route map with an optional prefix-list match and set command.
text.erzeugt_icmp_ip_sla_mit_track: Generates ICMP IP SLA with Track.
text.erzeugt_getrackte_primary_route_plu: Generates a tracked primary route and a floating backup route.
text.befehl_area_area_stub_area_area_stu:
Commands: `area <Area> stub`, `area <Area> stub no-summary`, `area <Area> nssa`, or `area <Area> nssa no-summary`.

Note: The area type must match on all routers in that area.
text.befehle_bei_md5_ip_ospf_authenticat:
Commands for MD5: `ip ospf authentication message-digest` and `ip ospf message-digest-key 1 md5 <Key>`.

Parameters: Interface = OSPF interface, md5/clear = authentication type, Key = shared key.
text.befehle_ip_ospf_hello_interval_seku:
Commands: `ip ospf hello-interval <Seconds>` and `ip ospf dead-interval <Seconds>`.

Note: OSPF neighbors must use matching timers.
text.befehl_area_area_range_netz_maske_e:
Command: `area <Area> range <Network> <Mask>`.

Use it for summarization on an ABR between OSPF areas.
text.neighbor_policy_neighbor_routemap_i: Neighbor policy: Neighbor|Route map in|Route map out|Prefix list in|Prefix list out|Keepalive|Hold
text.befehle_neighbor_ip_route_map_name_:
Commands: `neighbor <IP> route-map <Name> in|out`, `neighbor <IP> prefix-list <Name> in|out`, and `neighbor <IP> timers <Keepalive> <Hold>`.

Route maps and prefix lists must be defined separately.
text.befehle_address_family_ipv6_unicast:
Commands: `address-family ipv6 unicast`, `neighbor <IPv6> remote-as <AS>`, `neighbor <IPv6> activate`, `network <Prefix>`, and optional route maps for inbound/outbound policy.

Use this to enable BGP for IPv6 independently of IPv4.
text.aktiviert_cisco_express_forwarding_:
Enables Cisco Express Forwarding. MPLS requires CEF on classic IOS and IOS-XE platforms as the foundation for label switching.

Command:
ip cef
text.aktiviert_mpls_global_zusatzlich_mu:
Enables MPLS globally. MPLS must also be enabled on transit and core interfaces with `mpls ip`.

Command:
mpls ip
text.legt_das_label_distribution_protoko:
Sets the label-distribution protocol. LDP is the common default in modern IOS and IOS-XE designs.

Command:
mpls label protocol ldp
text.setzt_die_stabile_ldp_router_id_emp:
Sets a stable LDP router ID. A loopback interface reachable through the IGP is recommended.

Command:
mpls ldp router-id <Interface> [force]
text.erzwingt_die_gewahlte_ldp_router_id:
Forces the selected LDP router ID. Use `force` deliberately because existing LDP sessions may be rebuilt briefly.

Command component:
`force`
text.mpls_interfaces_interface_beschreib: MPLS interfaces: Interface|Optional description|Optional MPLS MTU|LDP-IGP Sync Yes/No
text.aktiviert_mpls_auf_transit_core_int:
Enables MPLS on transit and core interfaces. An MPLS MTU may optionally be set per interface. Leaving it empty is usually preferable when the platform already handles the overhead correctly or MTU is planned separately.

Command components:
interface <Interface> = core or transit interface.
description <Description> = optional documentation.
mpls ip = enables MPLS/LDP on the interface.
mpls mtu <Bytes> = optional and platform-dependent; do not set 9000 indiscriminately.
mpls ldp igp sync = optional; prevents blackholing while LDP and the IGP are not synchronized.

Format: Interface|Optional description|Optional MPLS MTU|LDP-IGP Sync Yes/No
text.setzt_optionale_ldp_nachbarpasswort:
Configures optional LDP neighbor passwords. Use this only when the peer is configured identically.

Command:
mpls ldp neighbor <Neighbor-IP> password <Password>
text.lokale_bgp_as_fur_mpls_l3vpn_mp_bgp:
Local BGP AS for MPLS L3VPN and MP-BGP. If left empty, no VPNv4 BGP configuration is generated.

Command:
router bgp <AS>
text.update_source_fur_vpnv4_peers_typis:
Update source for VPNv4 peers. Loopback0 is commonly used so the session remains independent of the physical link.

Command:
neighbor <Peer-IP> update-source <Interface>
text.vpnv4_peers_peer_ip_remote_as_besch: VPNv4 peers: Peer-IP|Remote-AS|Optional description|Send-Community extended Yes/No
text.erzeugt_mp_bgp_vpnv4_peers_fur_mpls:
Generates MP-BGP VPNv4 peers for MPLS L3VPN. `send-community extended` is normally required for route targets.

Command components:
neighbor <Peer-IP> remote-as <AS>
address-family vpnv4
 neighbor <Peer-IP> activate
 neighbor <Peer-IP> send-community extended

Format: Peer-IP|Remote-AS|Optional description|Send-Community extended Yes/No
text.l3vpn_vrfs_vrf_rd_rt_import_rt_expo: L3VPN VRFs: VRF|RD|RT Import|RT Export|description optional
text.erzeugt_vrf_definitionen_fur_mpls_l:
Creates VRF definitions for MPLS Layer 3 VPNs with an RD and route targets. If the VRF module is already in use, duplicate VRF definitions may be generated; the duplicate check reports them.

Command components:
vrf definition <VRF>
 rd <RD>
 route-target import <RT>
 route-target export <RT>
 address-family ipv4

Format: VRF|RD|Import RT|Export RT|Optional description
text.format_je_zeile_name_rd_beschreibun:
Format per line: Name|RD|Description.
Name = freely selectable VRF name that must be used exactly the same way for interfaces, static routes, and routing protocols.
RD = route distinguisher, for example 65000:10. It makes routes technically unique, especially when identical networks exist in multiple VRFs.
Description = optional text for documentation.
Export example:
ip vrf SERVER
 rd 65000:10
 description Server VRF
text.format_je_zeile_vlan_vrf_ipv4_maske:
Format per line: VLAN|VRF|IPv4|Mask|Reserved|Name|IPv6/Prefix|IPv6 enable.
VLAN = VLAN ID, for example 10.
VRF = name from the VRF definitions.
IPv4/Mask = gateway address for the VLAN inside this VRF.
Reserved = leave empty for compatibility with older templates.
Name = VLAN name.
IPv6/Prefix = optional IPv6 gateway, for example 2001:db8:10::1/64.
IPv6 enable = Yes when `ipv6 enable` should also be configured.
Export example:
interface Vlan10
 ip vrf forwarding SERVER
 ip address 192.168.10.1 255.255.255.0
text.befehl_ip_route_vrf_vrf_zielnetz_ma:
Command: ip route vrf <VRF> <Destination-Network> <Mask> <Next-Hop>.
VRF = routing table in which the route is installed.
Destination = target network, for example 0.0.0.0 for a default route.
Mask = subnet mask of the destination network.
Next hop = next router within the same VRF.
text.befehl_ipv6_route_vrf_vrf_ipv6_pref:
Command: ipv6 route vrf <VRF> <IPv6-Prefix> <Next-Hop>.
VRF = IPv6 routing table.
Destination/prefix = for example ::/0 for a default route or 2001:db8:20::/64.
Next hop = IPv6 address of the next router. A link-local next hop usually also requires an outgoing interface.
text.befehlsteile_vrf_routingtabelle_in_:
Command components:
VRF = routing table in which OSPF runs.
Process = local OSPF process ID; it does not need to match neighboring routers.
Network = IPv4 network activated by the network command.
Wildcard = inverse mask, for example 0.0.0.255 for /24.
Area = OSPF area, for example 0 for the backbone.
Router ID = optional but recommended; it must be unique within the OSPF domain.
Export example:
router ospf 10 vrf SERVER
 router-id 10.10.10.10
 network 192.168.10.0 0.0.0.255 area 0
text.befehlsteile_vrf_routingtabelle_fur:
Command components:
VRF = routing table for IPv6 OSPF.
Process = OSPFv3 process ID.
Reserved = leave empty for compatibility.
Router ID = OSPFv3 still requires an IPv4-formatted router ID.
Export example:
router ospfv3 10
 address-family ipv6 vrf SERVER
  router-id 10.10.10.10
text.befehl_interface_interface_ospfv3_p:
Command: interface <Interface> plus ospfv3 <Process> ipv6 area <Area>.
Interface = SVI or physical interface assigned to a VRF.
VRF = documentation and validation field; the actual VRF is inherited from the interface.
Process = must match the OSPFv3 process configured above.
Area = OSPFv3 area, usually 0 for the backbone.
text.befehlsteile_vrf_bgp_address_family:
Command components:
VRF = BGP address family, for example `address-family ipv4 vrf SERVER`.
AS = local BGP AS.
Neighbor = IPv4 address of the BGP neighbor in this VRF.
Remote AS = peer AS. The same AS indicates iBGP; a different AS indicates eBGP.
Network/mask = prefix to advertise. It normally must exist in the routing table.
text.befehlsteile_vrf_ipv6_address_famil:
Command components:
VRF = IPv6 address family of the VRF.
AS = local BGP AS.
Neighbor = IPv6 address of the peer.
Remote AS = peer AS.
Prefix = IPv6 network to advertise, for example 2001:db8:10::/64.
Export example:
router bgp 65000
 address-family ipv6 vrf SERVER
  neighbor 2001:db8:ffff::2 remote-as 65010
  neighbor 2001:db8:ffff::2 activate
  network 2001:db8:10::/64
text.ipv6_adresse_mit_prafix_z_b_2001_db: IPv6 address with prefix, for example 2001:db8:10::1/64. A link-local address can be set manually, which is particularly relevant for static routes through fe80:: addresses.
text.basis_dhcp_pool_fur_domain_lease_od: Basic DHCP pool. Use the extended pools below when a domain, lease time, or option 150 is required.
text.adressen_oder_bereiche_die_dhcp_nic: Addresses or ranges that the DHCP server must not assign.
text.erweiterter_dhcp_pool_mit_domain_le: Extended DHCP pool with domain, lease time, and option 150 for VoIP or TFTP.
text.extended_acl_quelle_und_ziel_im_ios: Extended ACL. Source and destination use IOS syntax. For example, destination 'any eq 22' permits SSH. ACLs filter traffic based on direction and interface.
text.weist_acl_einem_interface_zu_in_pru: Assigns an ACL to an interface. `in` filters traffic entering the interface; `out` filters traffic leaving the interface.
text.standard_acl_filtert_nur_nach_quell: A standard ACL filters only by source. It is commonly used for VTY or management access control.
text.extended_acl_filtert_nach_quelle_zi: An extended ACL filters by source, destination, protocol, and optionally a port.
text.weist_assistent_acls_interfaces_zu: Assigns ACLs created by the assistant to interfaces.
text.statische_ipv6_routen_auch_via_link: Creates static IPv6 routes, including routes using a link-local next hop and an outgoing interface.
text.weist_ipv6_acl_einem_interface_zu: Assigns an IPv6 ACL to an interface.
text.dhcpv6_relay_ziel_je_interface: Configures a DHCPv6 relay destination per interface.
text.bgp_ipv6_as_neighbor_remote_as_pref: BGP IPv6: AS|Neighbor|Remote AS|Prefix|Empty|Update source|Outbound route map
text.bgp_fur_ipv6_ohne_vrf_erzeugt_addre: BGP for IPv6 without VRF. Generates `address-family ipv6 unicast`, activates neighbors, and advertises IPv6 prefixes.
text.eigrp_fur_ipv6_ios_ios_xe_und_platt: EIGRP for IPv6 is IOS/IOS-XE and platform dependent. It requires `ipv6 unicast-routing` and activation on the relevant interfaces.
text.is_is_fur_ipv6_nutzt_address_family: IS-IS for IPv6. Uses address-family ipv6 and enables IS-IS on the interface. Useful in larger provider-style or core-style designs.
text.befehl_ip_access_list_extended_name: Command: `ip access-list extended <name>`. Enter the ACL name, for example MGMT-SSH.
text.permit_erlaubt_passenden_traffic_de: permit allowed relevant Traffic. deny blockiert relevant Traffic.
text.protokoll_der_acl_regel_ports_sind_: Protocol of the ACL rule. Port numbers are meaningful only for TCP or UDP.
text.any_jede_quelle_host_einzelne_ip_ne: any = any source. host = one source IP address. network = source network with a wildcard mask, for example 192.168.10.0 0.0.0.255.
text.bei_host_nur_ip_eintragen_bei_netz_: For a host, enter only the IP address. For a network, enter the network and wildcard mask. Leave empty for `any`.
text.any_jedes_ziel_host_einzelne_ziel_i: any = any destination. host = one destination IP address. network = destination network with wildcard mask.
text.port_wird_als_eq_port_am_ziel_ergan: Port is as eq <Port> on the target adds. only for TCP or UDP.
text.optional_wenn_gesetzt_wird_die_acl_: optional. when set, is the ACL with ip access-group on this Interface applied.
text.in_traffic_kommt_ins_interface_hine: in = traffic enters the interface. out = traffic leaves the interface.
text.optional_interface_range_die_als_un: Optional interface range to be disabled as unused, for example Gi0/10-24.
text.wahlt_welche_nat_art_exportiert_wir: Selects which NAT type is exported. This prevents multiple NAT variants from being generated accidentally. Use All only when multiple NAT methods are intentionally combined.
text.inside_internes_netz_outside_richtu: inside = internal network; outside = WAN/Internet direction. NAT requires correctly marked interfaces.
text.pat_nat_overload_ubersetzt_viele_in: PAT/NAT overload translates many internal source addresses to the outside interface address.
text.1_1_nat_zwischen_interner_und_globa: 1:1 NAT between an inside-local and an inside-global address.
text.portforward_protokoll_insidelocal_i: Port forwarding: Protocol|Inside local|Inside port|Inside global|Global port
text.grundlage_fur_nat_ausnahmen_z_b_vpn: Basis for NAT exemptions, for example VPN traffic.
text.gre_erstellt_einen_unverschlusselte: GRE creates an unencrypted tunnel between two endpoints. Combine GRE with IPsec when encryption is required.
text.fur_befehle_die_noch_kein_eigenes_m: For commands that do not yet have a dedicated module. They are copied to the export exactly as entered. Caution: this field is not syntax-validated.
text.erzeugt_basis_fur_gre_over_ipsec_mi: Generates the foundation for GRE over IPsec with an ISAKMP policy, transform set, crypto map, and tunnel interface.
text.ziel_fur_ip_sla_tracking: Destination monitored by IP SLA tracking.
text.erzeugt_einfache_dmz_interface_und_: Generates a basic DMZ interface and ACL foundation.
text.aktuelle_laufende_konfiguration_im_: current running configuration in the RAM.
text.gespeicherte_startkonfiguration_im_: stored startup configuration in the NVRAM.
text.ios_version_uptime_modell_seriennum: IOS version, uptime, model, serial number, and license.
text.hardware_inventory_seriennummern_mo: Hardware inventory, serial numbers, and modules.
text.interface_ip_adresse_status_protoco: Interface, IP address, status, and protocol.
text.ipv6_adressen_und_interface_status_: Compact IPv6 addresses and interface status.
text.interface_details_fehler_drops_crc_: interface details, error, Drops, CRC, bandwidth.
text.switchport_status_vlan_duplex_speed: Switch port status, VLAN, duplex, and speed.
text.gelernte_mac_adressen_je_vlan_port: Learned MAC addresses per VLAN and port.
text.stp_status_fur_bestimmtes_vlan: STP status for a specific VLAN.
text.switchport_modus_access_voice_vlan_: switchport mode, Access-/Voice-VLAN and QinQ/trunk details for a Port.
text.details_zu_einem_bestimmten_vlan_z_: Shows details for a specific VLAN, for example a data, voice, or provider VLAN.

text.zweck_ssh_zugriff_und_line_vty_zent:
Purpose:
Configure SSH access and VTY lines centrally.

Generated commands:
crypto key generate rsa, ip ssh version 2, line vty, login local/AAA, transport input ssh, and exec-timeout.

Important:
VTY configuration is generated only here to prevent duplicate line-vty sections.
text.zweck_authentifizierung_und_autoris:
Purpose:
Prepare authentication and authorization.

Generated commands:
aaa new-model, aaa authentication login default, and aaa authorization exec default.

Note:
Plan a local user as fallback for RADIUS/TACACS+.
text.zweck_vlans_und_svis_ohne_vrf_konfi:
Purpose:
Configure VLANs and SVIs without VRF.

Generated commands:
vlan, name, interface Vlan, ip address, and ipv6 address.

Note:
In VRF mode, use the VLAN / SVI VRF module instead.
text.zweck_vrfs_als_getrennte_routingtab:
Purpose:
Define VRFs as separate routing tables.

Generated commands:
ip vrf <Name>, rd <ASN:Number>, description.

Note:
Other VRF modules reference these names exactly.
text.zweck_svis_einer_vrf_zuordnen_erzeu:
Purpose:
SVIs a VRF zuordnen.

Generated commands:
interface VLAN, ip vrf forwarding, ip address, ipv6 address.

Important:
ip vrf forwarding must before ip address shows.
text.zweck_statische_ipv4_und_ipv6_route:
Purpose:
static IPv4- and IPv6-routes per VRF generate.

Generated commands:
ip route vrf ..., ipv6 route vrf ...
text.zweck_bgp_ohne_vrf_fur_ipv4_ipv6_ko:
Purpose:
Configure BGP for IPv4/IPv6 without VRF.

Generated commands:
router bgp, neighbor remote-as, network, and address-family ipv6.
text.zweck_nat_pat_auf_routern_konfiguri:
Purpose:
Configure NAT/PAT on routers.

Generated commands:
ip nat inside/outside and ip nat inside source ...

Important:
Inside and outside interfaces must be assigned correctly.
